feedback.text: Does this initiative include video games? Developers and publishers can't support their games forever. But they often don't allow consumers to continue playing after support has ended. It feels like a remote "kill switch" that prevents consumer from playing their games. At the very least, consumers should be informed of how long they can expect to retain access to their games. Ideally, consumers should be able to continue playing games they purchased, even without support from the publisher.
